blockingIssues.description: Swap execution still uses wallet signAndSubmitTransaction with a helper-built payload instead of SDK CashOrderbook.placeOrder().
Order form submission still uses wallet signAndSubmitTransaction with a helper-built payload instead of SDK CashOrderbook.placeOrder().
web/lib/sdk.ts introduces a custom payload builder path and does not instantiate/use CashOrderbook.placeOrder(), leaving the original SDK-submission blocker unresolved.

appliedUpdates.description: Added .factory/library/frontend-wallet-sdk-write-pattern.md documenting the current wallet-adapter vs SDK write-path mismatch and why scrutiny must verify the required submission path.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update frontend-worker procedure to include a concrete SDK-first wallet-adapter pattern for write calls and make explicit when direct signAndSubmitTransaction is allowed.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: fix-frontend-sdk-integration still submitted via signAndSubmitTransaction in swap/order form paths because current skill guidance points workers to direct wallet submission without a compliant SDK-write flow pattern.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add an explicit frontend convention that trading transactions must follow the required SDK submission path (or a documented waiver) to avoid repeated raw payload re-implementations.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Architecture expects frontend -> SDK -> RPC, but scrutiny round 1 and rerun still found wallet direct-submission implementations for swap/order flows.
